description: Auditable classification of installed @hcengineering SDK model exports for MCP sdk-parity tracking. Every discovered plugin class or exported model-like declaration must be covered, marked as a gap, ignored, or classified as not MCP-facing.

referenceModelAreas.rationale: These reference model areas correspond to current first-class MCP tool categories, although individual installed SDK exports can still be partial in the export ledger below.

referenceModelAreas.matrixLocation: Missing Or Partially Missing SDK Surfaces
referenceModelAreas.rationale: These reference model areas are represented by explicit rows in the feature gap matrix.

These reference model areas are platform plumbing, UI/editor/presentation metadata, server-only model support, or build/upload/export infrastructure rather than first-class LLM-facing resources today.

groups.rationale: Current activity tools expose activity list/get/pin, generic replies, references, filters, reactions, saved messages, and mentions as first-class MCP operations.
Remaining activity exports are presenters, viewlets, extension/provider metadata, controls for server-generated activity filtering, or mixin metadata rather than first-class LLM-facing resources.

Generic attachment CRUD/download/pin, class-selecting upload for Attachment/Embedding/Photo, saved attachments, drawing CRUD, and issue/document convenience attachment tools cover the safe attachment models.

Current board tools cover board discovery, board create/update/archive, board card list/get/create/update, workflow status/type resolution, assignees, members, location, cover, dates, archived-card deletion, board labels, saved views, menu pages, viewlets, and common board preference reads. Provider integrations and board deletion remain deferred.
Board card cover values are exposed through board card create/update fields. The CardCover SDK export is the underlying type metadata rather than a separate LLM-facing resource.
Calendar tools cover event CRUD, recurring events/instances, stable event fields, participant mutation, calendar target moves, writable calendar discovery, and schedule CRUD.

Calendar configuration, primary-calendar preferences, external calendars, presenter metadata, and invite/RSVP response state are tracked as remaining gaps. Current 0.7.0 calendar declarations expose no RSVP response model.

Read-only virtual-office tools cover floors, rooms, offices, transient room info, transient participant info, meeting minutes, device preferences, and room recording/transcription defaults. Meeting and MeetingSchedule mixin data is exposed as room-aware calendar event/schedule summaries instead of raw mixin internals.

Live meeting join/leave/start/stop writes, meeting schedule room writes, transcripts/recordings as first-class artifacts, and room/floor/office CRUD are deferred until transient session/LiveKit behavior and storage-backed recording/transcript models are implemented in a safe integration slice.
Current card tools cover card spaces, master tags, and card CRUD.
Card favorites, roles, sections, editor sections, and advanced tag/schema metadata remain matrix gaps.

Current channel and direct-message tools cover channels, channel messages, one-to-one DM create/list/message list/send/update/delete, thread replies, channel member list/add/remove, join/leave, archive/unarchive, conversation star/closed state, and group direct-message create.
Chat viewlets, sync metadata, object chat panels, request-access flows if modeled, pinned messages, message attachments, and translation remain matrix gaps.
Current contacts tools expose person, organization, employee/member, and organization-channel operations.
Contact channels, identities, providers, statuses, person spaces, user profiles, and related contact administration remain matrix gaps.
Avatar, UI tab, translation preference, and low-level user-role support objects are not currently treated as first-class LLM-facing MCP resources.

groups.matrixLocation: Core classes, attributes, enums; Core spaces, permissions, roles; #92 SDK discovery phase 2
groups.rationale: Existing tools expose user statuses, full-text search, blobs through storage/download flows, generic association/relation discovery/mutation helpers, class/interface/mixin, attribute, enum, plugin configuration, domain index configuration, sequence, and space type capability discovery.
Generic collaborator tools list/add/remove core.class.Collaborator rows for arbitrary issue/document/raw object notification subscriptions.

groups.matrixLocation: Core classes, attributes, enums; Core spaces, permissions, roles
groups.rationale: Remaining core write-side configuration, role/permission definition writes, generic space creation, class collaborator metadata, statuses, and write-side model management are represented as matrix gaps. Generic space discovery, space type/permission reads, safe existing-space metadata updates, member mutations, owner replacement, typed-space role member mutations, object collaborators, read-only plugin configuration, domain index configuration, and sequence discovery are covered.
Core primitive model infrastructure, transaction classes, type wrappers, and versioning internals are not LLM-facing product resources by themselves.

Current document tools cover non-controlled document teamspaces, document CRUD/content operations, and read-only snapshot/history listing plus markdown retrieval. Snapshot restore and document PDF/export remain deferred.

Stale installed SDK declaration: current platform source removed the SavedDocument model registration and document-resource star/unstar usage in hcengineering/platform#10124. Current document stars are rating-backed DocReaction / ReactionKind.Star, tracked under the Rating / reputation gap.

Current inventory tools cover Category, Product, and Variant as first-class category hierarchy, product, variant/SKU CRUD operations, plus product-scoped photo, attachment, comment, and activity wrappers. Category/variant discussion wrappers remain out of the product-focused slice rather than missing inventory SDK model exports.

Current notification tools cover inbox notifications, doc notify contexts including context archive/unarchive, notification providers/types/settings, and current-account object subscribe/unsubscribe. Generic collaborator tools cover the underlying core collaborator rows.
Browser/push records, provider defaults, group/preference UI metadata, and the legacy collaborator array are UI/client preference plumbing or superseded by core.class.Collaborator tools.
Notification presenters, previews, and viewlets are UI/resource metadata rather than first-class MCP resources.
Generic space preference discovery/update remains a matrix gap.
Generic preference rows are broad SDK infrastructure. Use module-specific wrappers such as get_board_common_preference or the viewlet preference configs returned by list_viewlets.
SDK-level generic tag elements, references, and categories are covered; matrix gaps are now module-specific wrappers above the generic tag model.

Read-only message template discovery tools expose global template categories, message template summaries/details, template fields, placeholder field IDs, and field category summaries. Write, render/copy, and provider execution semantics remain tracked in the matrix row.
Current task-management and tracker tools expose safe project/task type and task/project surfaces through higher-level issue/project workflows.
Descriptor and class-level task/project configuration remains a matrix gap because writes can affect workspace-wide model behavior.
Ranking and presenter/class support objects are implementation metadata unless promoted through a concrete user workflow.

Current time and planner tools cover work slots, issue time reporting, Planner ToDo list/get/create/update/delete, complete/reopen, schedule/unschedule, owner, due date, priority, visibility, and personal/issue attachment support.
Document-attached action items, automation helper visibility, and richer team planner/reporting remain matrix gaps.
Presenter and priority type support objects are not first-class MCP resources by themselves.

Current view tools cover read-only saved filtered view discovery/get operations across modules plus viewlet descriptor and ViewletPreference configuration discovery. View and preference writes remain deferred.
Action metadata, reusable filters, reusable sorts, and reusable grouping/aggregation configuration remain matrix gaps.

View presenters, editors, validators, link providers, object factories, headers, tooltips, and list/panel UI extension points are client presentation metadata rather than first-class LLM-facing resources.

Current project, issue, component, milestone, issue-template, status, relation, related-issue target, project target preference, and time-reporting tools cover core tracker resources. GitHub sync metadata, PDF export, saved views, and broader workflow automation remain deferred.
Tracker type wrappers and the tracker-local Document support type are implementation metadata unless promoted through a concrete workflow.
